opp.name: SMRT_S2P
opp.next_step: 50% Confidence 6th Jun- ck to re-engage Kevin (though he is NOT) the evaluation owner (is good intel guy)- sending Horizon invite 4th May sent invite directly to Kevin at SMRT Apr 27 no update and unresponsive. Invitation to RT with no confirmation. RFP still expected but unclear when and what? KIV Apr 13: Partners' intel confirms RFP to come soon by April (IBM, Kpmg). Collaterals & RT invitation. Mar 13- RFP expected in coming 2 weeks. RFP mainly focused on S4H + procurement platform in the mix still to be confirmed. IBM & Kpmg are not able to confirm (+ direct engagement with no response so far). If they move forward with procurement, Zycus well positioned. Mar 04 Dropped connect email to Surekha 4th Feb-Fresh Intel is RFP coming in Q1-Temasek investing across SMRT in 2026 (SI's are not across current status). This is high profile opportunity now we will likely align with KPMG- We have limited key contacts- in procurement (some have left since last year) CPO remains the key influencer- has pro Ariba sentiment- though it is know Ariba WILL be replaced in 2026 Jan 12 Feedback from SIs confirms SMRT is happy with Ariba and they may not even launch an RFP. Pushing for exec meeting to clarify. Dec 08th Exec meeting with IT & Procurement by Dec 16th. IT as main project sponsor. Flanking strategy against SAP (complementarity) 07th Nov: RFP is postponed until early next year. SMRT requests for a meeting to review on I2O and ST Eng use case (to happen by Dec 10th). 12nd Sep: Meeting by end Sep to engage on recent wins and on AI roadmap 18th Aug: RFP postponed to Nov due to internal ERP project priorities. 07th Jul: Working with partners to identify the ones we can respond with. Lack of visibility (intel: SMRT can make the same decision as MOF, Ivalua) 23rd Jun: Checking with partners (IBM, Accenture, Kpmg) their expectations to respond with. 16th Jun: RFP expected end of July/beg Aug. Launch a BOFU 07th May: BD spoke with Kevin. Planning to launch RFP by May. To be checked and align with IBM 05th Mar: IBM not able to reengage and don't see any Opp to replace Ariba at short-term. To launch some marketing in Gen AI/ ease of use vs Ariba + to build champions. KIV and move to Q4. 03rd Mar: Shared detailed integration architecture with IBM & prospect: no feedback so far 20th Jan: Working with IBM on collaterals to share with IT and request for meeting before RFP to influence content. Presentation to be sent by Jan 24. 06th Jan: IBM confirms still under final approval to launch RFP by end Q1. IBM worked with IT to list some pain points around integration. Presales to prepare iSaaS differentiators to present by end of Jan. 04th Dec: No new update from Kevin. RPF expected by Q1 25. Request for session on integration (Checking with IT). Meet Kevin beg of Jan 18th Nov: Pending confirmation meeting with CPO. Call with Kevin (project manager by Tues 19) 11st Nov: Pending meeting with IT on integration and with Head of procurement (Ann Kok) by Nov 30. RFP Q1 25. 04th Nov: Short list against Ariba. IBM trying to influence RFP (IBM session on integration mainly this week). RFP to be launched before end of 24 01st Nov: Call with IBM on Mon04 to update on next steps and expected Tender date. 25th Oct: Request for pricing model on apps (pending meeting to clarify). Tender will be launched but still not clear if Q3 or Q4 (Budget approval is confirmed). 21st Oct: Review with IBM on tender date expectation. Contact with our champion this week. sent Plan 24 invitation 11th Oct: Kevin confirms procurement finished its evaluation on S2P platform and Zycus is one of the best rated internally. Still unclear how and when IT will launch tender. check again by mid oct. 04th Oct: Reaching again but no answer (Kenneth just left Smrt). BD to send collateral on Forrester Wave. 27th Sep: Reaching our champions for update but no answer yet. 20th Sep: IBM confirms Smrt still unclear on contractual process (standalone S2P RFP or part of larger ERP RFP). Compelling event is not clear 13th Sep: Carl reached out to Kevin and Ken for a catch-up coffee 17/18th 4th Sep: . Still engaging with PM to clarify some requirements from RFI. . Meeting with IBM next week to align on next steps and join approach to Procurement head. . Prospect confirming RFP should come by end of Sep 27th Aug: BD sending WP on SAP integration and solution value awareness. Guillaume engaging with IBM to update and align on executive engagement (Head of procurement). Answering additional questions about extended apps (due date Aug 30) Meeting with pm to update on process and expected rfp (Sep 05) 19th Aug- Follow up mail on pending requirement to clarify. 16th Aug Still working on RFP. Expect to be launched by end of Sep. Re-alignment with IBM next week. 01st Aug: Confirmation RFP for S2P will be launched in few months (Expected by Sep). RFP for ERP is a separate initiative. Currently clarifying on RFI. 29th July: IT focused on driving RFP for ERP (Decision to be made by December). Then, will launch RFP for S2P (Still unclear if sequential or in parallel RFPs). Request for an exec meeting with Head of Proc. Must align with IBM. 24th July- Looking for engaging with PM to update on RFP and timeline 17th Still internal activity- no change 4th July- RFP imminent- follow up with Kevin for full process timelines 21st June- F2F session yesterday- went very well- scripted scenarios. F/Up another deep dive on 24th (Tanvi). RFP scheduled for July- close date may push 7th June- Ken/Kevin have called to arrange 2 follow up workshops (20th/24th June on site. Great rapport and understanding of solution issues. SAP integration key. Leaning towards Zycus early. 29th May- Call from Kevin Tan (champion), presentation great, and want to set up a deep dive- will revert with proposed agenda focus. Process will accelerate expect to award Sept timeframe 23rd May- F2F Demo was conducted 26th April - RFI last date of submission has been extended from 2nd May 2024 to 16th May 2024. 24th Apri- Demo set for 23rd May'24. Positive feedback from CKs intro call. Sent across a list of info they are looking forward to, during the in-person meeting. 4th April, 2024 - Meeting set on calendar for 19th April, 2024 2nd April - Reply received from Ken with volume-related estimates; discussions on virtual meeting date set-up March, 2024 27th Mar - Qualification call taken with Kenneth Sim; confirmed they are evaluating vendors; asked for additional info on volume-related estimates 25th Mar - Received website demo request
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 5 Mar 2025 rep note: 'IBM not able to reengage and don't see any Opp to replace Ariba at short-term. To launch some marketing in Gen AI/ ease of use vs Ariba + to build champions.' Per the 12 Sep rep note: 'Meeting by end Sep to engage on recent wins and on AI roadmap.' These are Zycus-side strategic positioning around GenAI marketing and AI-roadmap engagement ‚Äî not yet a buyer-stated AI demand from SMRT.
• On 4 Feb 2026: 'Fresh Intel is RFP coming in Q1-Temasek investing across SMRT in 2026 (SI's are not across current status). This is high profile opportunity now we will likely align with KPMG- We have limited key contacts- in procurement (some have left since last year) CPO remains the key influencer- has pro Ariba sentiment- though it is know Ariba WILL be replaced in 2026.' On 13 Mar 2026: 'RFP expected in coming 2 weeks. RFP mainly focused on S4H + procurement platform in the mix.' On 4 May 2026: 'sent invite directly to Kevin at SMRT.' Buyer is anchored in S/4HANA + Ariba-replacement, not AI.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,412 chars over 18+ months). Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope, RFP is anticipated by Apr/May 2026. The 2 AI keywords are both Zycus rep-side positioning ('GenAI marketing', 'AI roadmap engagement'); CPO is pro-Ariba. Score 5 (boundary DOWN) reflects AI products in scope without buyer-originated AI ask, with a long Ariba-incumbent shadow.

items.quote: Zycus was not included in the invited vendors? Is there any way to confirm this
no Avoma calls found for this opp

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been articulated by the buyer across 24 months. The record carries only qualitative pain (SAP/Ariba integration pain points listed with IT, Next Step 2025-01-06; 'SAP integration key', 2024-06-07) with no cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figures stated by a SMRT stakeholder. Temasek is reportedly investing across SMRT in 2026 (Next Step 2026-02-04), which implies CFO-level financial governance the deal has never engaged. Without a quantified Ariba-versus-Zycus replacement TCO, there is nothing to justify a half-million-dollar platform swap to an approving executive. Move: co-build a quantified Ariba-replacement TCO and ROI model with IT and Procurement.

champion.narrative: There is no active champion. Kevin Tan (Senior Procurement Manager) was called a champion in 2024 ('Call from Kevin Tan (champion), presentation great', Next Step 2024-05-29) but is now explicitly 'NOT the evaluation owner (is good intel guy)' (Next Step 2026-06-06) and has been unresponsive to recent invites. Sim Kenneth (Senior Project Engineer, Evaluator) was an early advocate but left SMRT (Next Step 2024-10-04). 'We have limited key contacts in procurement (some have left since last year)' (Next Step 2026-02-04). No replacement champion with decision-owner access has emerged, and IT Decision Maker Jason Chin has never been engaged directly. Move: develop Jason Chin (IT) or a new procurement contact into a mandated champion, or secure a C-suite sponsor.

sources: SF Task 2026-06-22 (RFP released, Zycus inclusion unknown)
competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent and the only named competitor; this is a replacement play, not greenfield. Ariba enjoys active internal advocacy from the CPO ('CPO remains the key influencer- has pro Ariba sentiment', Next Step 2026-02-04) and SI-reported satisfaction ('SMRT is happy with Ariba and may not even launch an RFP', 2025-01-12), even though it is 'known Ariba WILL be replaced in 2026'. Zycus was 'one of the best rated internally' in the 2024 review (Next Step 2024-10-11) and shortlisted against Ariba (2024-11-04), but the 22 Jun 2026 signal that an RFP may have released without Zycus means Ariba (or status-quo inertia) is currently winning. Wedge: S/4HANA-native integration (iSaaS) plus Merlin AI against Ariba's legacy UX, activated only if Zycus gets into the tender.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is real but unevenly felt and unquantified. IT owns clear integration pain: IBM 'worked with IT to list some pain points around integration' (Next Step 2025-01-06) and 'SAP integration key' recurs as the technical driver (2024-06-07). Procurement, however, is comparatively satisfied: SI feedback says 'SMRT is happy with Ariba and may not even launch an RFP' and the CPO 'has pro Ariba sentiment' (Next Step 2025-01-12, 2026-02-04). So the pain owner is IT (Jason Chin, Surekha Kulkarni), while the budget-controlling function (Procurement) does not feel enough pain to force change. Move: unify the IT integration pain with a procurement cost/compliance case tied to the Temasek modernization mandate.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown because the deal has never reached commercials. Procurement is running a formal tender (email 2026-03-16), and the RFP was reportedly to cover S/4HANA plus the procurement platform (Next Step 2026-03-13), which ties the S2P paper process to a larger ERP initiative and to SI-led scoping. No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel detail has surfaced, and no contract-expiration forcing function is on record. The tender document itself would define the paper process. Move: obtain the RFP/tender terms to map submission, evaluation, and award mechanics.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Ann Huat Koh, Jason Chin = Decision Maker)
SF Next_Step__c 2025-12-08 (IT as main project sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or engaged in 24 months. Budget approval was noted in Oct 2024 (Next Step 2024-10-25: 'Budget approval is confirmed') but the approving executive was never named. Two Decision Makers hold Salesforce roles: Ann Huat Koh (Head, Procurement, primary) and Jason Chin (Director Information Technology), and IT was flagged as the main project sponsor (Next Step 2025-12-08), but neither has been converted to a bought-in budget sponsor and Koh is pro-Ariba. With Temasek investing across SMRT in 2026, real budget authority likely sits above procurement at a C-suite level Zycus has never reached. Move: identify and reach the tender's approving executive via IT (Jason Chin) or the partner channel.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal tender process gated by procurement, and it has explicitly locked Zycus out of the pre-RFP phase: 'we are preparing for an upcoming tender and are unable to engage vendors or facilitate introductions ahead of the formal process' (email received 2026-03-16). System integrators (IBM, KPMG, Accenture) sit between Zycus and the buyer and have been the primary intel channel throughout. The 22 Jun 2026 email suggests the RFP may have released, and the open question is whether Zycus is an invited vendor. The process is buyer- and SI-controlled with Zycus currently outside it. Move: use IBM/KPMG to confirm RFP status and secure inclusion.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are inferred from the deal history rather than a current buyer statement. In the 2024 evaluation SAP/S4HANA integration was the decisive criterion ('SAP integration key. Leaning towards Zycus early', Next Step 2024-06-07) and Zycus was 'one of the best rated internally' after procurement's platform review (Next Step 2024-10-11). The current tender is 'mainly focused on S4H + procurement platform in the mix' (Next Step 2026-03-13), so S/4HANA fit and procurement-suite breadth remain the core criteria. These are 2024-2026 rep-logged signals, not confirmed on a recent buyer call, so they are partial. Move: obtain the RFP document to confirm the scored criteria.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — SAP Ariba is the sole named competitor and the entrenched incumbent, currently the strongest threat: the CPO is pro-Ariba (Next Step 2026-02-04), SIs report SMRT is satisfied with Ariba (2025-01-12), and the 22 Jun 2026 email suggests a tender released without Zycus, meaning Ariba is winning on inertia. Zycus rated highly in the 2024 informal review (2024-10-11) but that evidence is stale. No other vendor (Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer) has been named in any source. We win only by getting into the RFP and pairing S/4HANA integration with a quantified Ariba-replacement TCO.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: SMRT Corporation Ltd — Formal Evaluation · $500K · closes 14 Nov

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 20 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 9 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 4 is anchored on the stage win (20), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 9 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5 / AIS_Status Moderate. Merlin is in product scope but the AI appetite is rep-manufactured, not buyer-stated; with no Avoma calls there is no evidence the AI narrative has resonated. Treated as curious, not hungry.

business_case.evidence: Only qualitative integration pain on record; no cost, savings, or ROI quantified by any SMRT stakeholder across 24 months.

items.change: RFP reportedly released; Zycus asked to confirm whether it was excluded from invited vendors
Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation; owner changed to Carl Kimball
Buyer declined to engage vendors ahead of the formal tender

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is negative. The stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 2026-06-06 and ownership moved to Carl Kimball the same day, but the underlying buyer engagement has cooled: the last two-way contact was the 16 Mar 2026 email in which SMRT declined to engage vendors pre-tender, and the 22 Jun 2026 email flags that an RFP may have released without Zycus invited. Amount was cut from 750k to 500k (2026-04-24) and close pushed to 2026-11-14 (2026-02-04).

The deal was cut from 750,000 to 500,000 in April with no quantified value case to defend the spend.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cold. The only two-way buyer contact in 2026 was the 16 Mar email declining engagement; every touch since is one-way rep outreach with no reply, and the tender may have moved without Zycus.

north_star_verdict.math: 134 days to close (2026-11-14). Deal created 2024-04-09, 815 days old, in Formal Evaluation since 2026-06-06 after 24+ months in Qualified. Last buyer-initiated contact was the 16 Mar 2026 email declining to engage vendors ahead of the formal process. All activity since (23 Apr, 15 Jun, 22 Jun) is one-way Clari email with no confirmed reply. The 22 Jun email asks whether an RFP released and Zycus was excluded, implying the tender has moved without us. RFP has been postponed and re-forecast repeatedly since Jul 2024. No submission-date series. No compelling event owned by Zycus. Closing in 134 days from a cold, possibly-excluded position is not credible.
evidence: POSSIBLE RFP EXCLUSION: Task 2026-06-22 (rep email): 'we understand an RFP has been released for SMRT, and Zycus was not included in the invited vendors? Is there any way to confirm this'. The tender may have moved without Zycus.

BUYER DECLINED ENGAGEMENT: Task 2026-03-16 (email received): SMRT is 'preparing for an upcoming tender and unable to engage vendors or facilitate introductions ahead of the formal process.' Last two-way buyer contact.
COLD: Only one-way rep outreach since 16 Mar 2026 (23 Apr AI World Tour invites, 15 Jun Horizon invite, 22 Jun status check) with no confirmed buyer reply.
CPO BLOCKER: Ann Huat Koh (Head, Procurement) has pro-Ariba sentiment (Next Step 2026-02-04). SI feedback Jan 2025: 'SMRT is happy with Ariba and may not even launch an RFP' (Next Step 2025-01-12).

NO ECONOMIC BUYER / NO CHAMPION: Kevin Tan explicitly NOT the evaluation owner (Next Step 2026-06-06); Sim Kenneth left SMRT (Next Step 2024-10-04); no C-suite or Temasek-level sponsor mapped in 24 months.
FORECAST INDEFENSIBLE: Pipeline forecast with no live buyer engagement, no confirmed RFP inclusion, no EB, no champion, and internal Ariba advocacy is not credible.
north_star_verdict.headline: An RFP appears to have released without Zycus invited (email 22 Jun), the buyer refused pre-tender engagement (16 Mar), and there is no economic buyer, no active champion, and a pro-Ariba CPO. 134 days to close on a Pipeline deal that has gone cold.

flags: Zycus may have been left off the released RFP (22 Jun email); confirm inclusion through Kevin Tan and IBM/KPMG this week before anything else.
No economic buyer and no active champion in 24 months; multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder Jason Chin and to a Temasek-level sponsor rather than staying single-threaded through procurement.
The CPO is pro-Ariba; a quantified Ariba-replacement TCO tied to the S/4HANA program is the only lever that shifts procurement off the incumbent.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a cold, possibly-excluded tender.

customer_preference.evidence: CPO pro-Ariba (Next Step 2026-02-04) and SI feedback 'SMRT is happy with Ariba' (2025-01-12); no recent Zycus-specific pull. The favourable 'Zycus one of the best rated' signal is stale (2024-10-11).

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the sole named competitor and the entrenched incumbent, currently the strongest threat: the CPO is pro-Ariba (Next Step 2026-02-04), SIs report SMRT is satisfied with Ariba (2025-01-12), and the 22 Jun 2026 email suggests a tender released without Zycus, meaning Ariba is winning on inertia. Zycus rated highly in the 2024 informal review (2024-10-11) but that evidence is stale. No other vendor (Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer) has been named in any source. We win only by getting into the RFP and pairing S/4HANA integration with a quantified Ariba-replacement TCO.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: CPO remains the key influencer- has pro Ariba sentiment- though it is known Ariba WILL be replaced in 2026
competitors.source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-02-04; SF Competitors__c; SF Task 2026-06-22
competitors.how_we_win: S/4HANA-native integration (iSaaS) plus Merlin AI against Ariba's legacy UX; get invited to the tender and lead with a quantified Ariba-replacement TCO.

items.deliverable: Confirm the released RFP scope and whether Zycus is an invited vendor
items.grounding_quote: we understand an RFP has been released for SMRT, and Zycus was not included in the invited vendors? Is there any way to confirm this

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 5 (Moderate). Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope and Zycus has pushed a GenAI/agentic narrative (AI World Tour invites, 23 Apr 2026), but every AI signal on record is Zycus-side positioning; the buyer has never originated an AI ask and is anchored on S/4HANA plus Ariba replacement. The AI story is not landing.

items.quote: RFP mainly focused on S4H + procurement platform in the mix
procurement finished its evaluation on S2P platform and Zycus is one of the best rated internally
SMRT is happy with Ariba and they may not even launch an RFP

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's stated criteria (S/4HANA integration, a full procurement-suite platform) align with Zycus strengths, but the incumbent-satisfaction dimension is exposed: procurement is comfortable with Ariba and Zycus may be outside the current tender.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 5 Mar 2025 rep note: 'IBM not able to reengage and don't see any Opp to replace Ariba at short-term. To launch some marketing in Gen AI/ ease of use vs Ariba + to build champions.' Per the 12 Sep rep note: 'Meeting by end Sep to engage on recent wins and on AI roadmap.' These are Zycus-side strategic positioning around GenAI marketing and AI-roadmap engagement ‚Äî not yet a buyer-stated AI demand from SMRT.
• On 4 Feb 2026: 'Fresh Intel is RFP coming in Q1-Temasek investing across SMRT in 2026 (SI's are not across current status). This is high profile opportunity now we will likely align with KPMG- We have limited key contacts- in procurement (some have left since last year) CPO remains the key influencer- has pro Ariba sentiment- though it is know Ariba WILL be replaced in 2026.' On 13 Mar 2026: 'RFP expected in coming 2 weeks. RFP mainly focused on S4H + procurement platform in the mix.' On 4 May 2026: 'sent invite directly to Kevin at SMRT.' Buyer is anchored in S/4HANA + Ariba-replacement, not AI.
• Confidence is Medium given the unusually rich NextStep (6,412 chars over 18+ months). Merlin and Merlin Intake are in product scope, RFP is anticipated by Apr/May 2026. The 2 AI keywords are both Zycus rep-side positioning ('GenAI marketing', 'AI roadmap engagement'); CPO is pro-Ariba. Score 5 (boundary DOWN) reflects AI products in scope without buyer-originated AI ask, with a long Ariba-incumbent shadow.

Jan 12 Feedback from SIs confirms SMRT is happy with Ariba and they may not even launch an RFP. Pushing for exec meeting to clarify.

Dec 08th Exec meeting with IT & Procurement by Dec 16th. IT as main project sponsor. Flanking strategy against SAP (complementarity)

07th Nov: RFP is postponed until early next year. SMRT requests for a meeting to review on I2O and ST Eng use case (to happen by Dec 10th).

12nd Sep: Meeting by end Sep to engage on recent wins and on AI roadmap

18th Aug: RFP postponed to Nov due to internal ERP project priorities.

07th Jul: Working with partners to identify the ones we can respond with. Lack of visibility (intel: SMRT can make the same decision as MOF, Ivalua)

23rd Jun: Checking with partners (IBM, Accenture, Kpmg) their expectations to respond with.

16th Jun: RFP expected end of July/beg Aug. Launch a BOFU

07th May: BD spoke with Kevin. Planning to launch RFP by May. To be checked and align with IBM

05th Mar: IBM not able to reengage and don't see any Opp to replace Ariba at short-term. To launch some marketing in Gen AI/ ease of use vs Ariba + to build champions. KIV and move to Q4.

03rd Mar: Shared detailed integration architecture with IBM & prospect: no feedback so far

20th Jan: Working with IBM on collaterals to share with IT and request for meeting before RFP to influence content. Presentation to be sent by Jan 24.

06th Jan: IBM confirms still under final approval to launch RFP by end Q1. IBM worked with IT to list some pain points around integration. Presales to prepare iSaaS differentiators to present by end of Jan.

04th Dec: No new update from Kevin. RPF expected by Q1 25. Request for session on integration (Checking with IT). Meet Kevin beg of Jan

18th Nov: Pending confirmation meeting with CPO. Call with Kevin (project manager by Tues 19)

11st Nov: Pending meeting with IT on integration and with Head of procurement (Ann Kok) by Nov 30. RFP Q1 25.

04th Nov: Short list against Ariba. IBM trying to influence RFP (IBM session on integration mainly this week). RFP to be launched before end of 24

01st Nov: Call with IBM on Mon04 to update on next steps and expected Tender date.

25th Oct: Request for pricing model on apps (pending meeting to clarify). Tender will be launched but still not clear if Q3 or Q4 (Budget approval is confirmed).

21st Oct: Review with IBM on tender date expectation. Contact with our champion this week. sent Plan 24 invitation

11th Oct: Kevin confirms procurement finished its evaluation on S2P platform and Zycus is one of the best rated internally. Still unclear how and when IT will launch tender. check again by mid oct.

04th Oct: Reaching again but no answer (Kenneth just left Smrt). BD to send collateral on Forrester Wave.

27th Sep: Reaching our champions for update but no answer yet.

20th Sep: IBM confirms Smrt still unclear on contractual process (standalone S2P RFP or part of larger ERP RFP). Compelling event is not clear

4th Sep:

. Still engaging with PM to clarify some requirements from RFI.

Answering additional questions about extended apps (due date Aug 30) Meeting with pm to update on process and expected rfp (Sep 05)

19th Aug- Follow up mail on pending requirement to clarify.

16th Aug Still working on RFP. Expect to be launched by end of Sep. Re-alignment with IBM next week.

01st Aug: Confirmation RFP for S2P will be launched in few months (Expected by Sep). RFP for ERP is a separate initiative. Currently clarifying on RFI.

29th July: IT focused on driving RFP for ERP (Decision to be made by December). Then, will launch RFP for S2P (Still unclear if sequential or in parallel RFPs). Request for an exec meeting with Head of Proc. Must align with IBM.

24th July- Looking for engaging with PM to update on RFP and timeline

17th Still internal activity- no change

4th July- RFP imminent- follow up with Kevin for full process timelines

21st June- F2F session yesterday- went very well- scripted scenarios. F/Up another deep dive on 24th (Tanvi). RFP scheduled for July- close date may push

7th June- Ken/Kevin have called to arrange 2 follow up workshops (20th/24th June on site. Great rapport and understanding of solution issues. SAP integration key. Leaning towards Zycus early.

29th May- Call from Kevin Tan (champion), presentation great, and want to set up a deep dive- will revert with proposed agenda focus. Process will accelerate expect to award Sept timeframe
